subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Comprehensive and wellâjustified selection of a broad range of relevant secondary sources and theoretical tools
Clear, accurate and effective communication of the topicâs scope and significance with quantitative evidence and visual support
Research question is explicitly stated, narrowly focused and consistently linked to the discussion throughout
Methodology is described in detail with evidence of informed selection of analytical frameworks
weaknesses: Acknowledged limitations of secondary research (bias, lack of primary data) are not mitigated through triangulation or alternative methods
Minor repetition in company background and occasional spelling lapses (e.g. âMarriotâ)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors of the top band by communicating the topic accurately and comprehensively, formulating a well-focused research question, and demonstrating a clearly informed methodological design.  Although minor polish issues and lack of explicit bias-mitigation keep it from flawless perfection, the overall performance warrants the full 6 marks under Focus and Method.

strengths: Consistently appropriate and relevant application of source material with wellâintegrated quotations, statistics and graphs
Clear and coherent demonstration of subject knowledge through correct definitions and contextual explanations of business concepts
Terminology (âdynamic pricing,â âmarket penetration,â âgross profit margin,â etc.) is used accurately and effectively to support arguments
weaknesses: A small number of claims rely uncritically on opinion pieces and blogs without scrutiny of source reliability
Occasional conceptual imprecision (e.g. service vs. product innovation) prevents entirely consistent mastery
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Most evidence is applied clearly and appropriately and the candidate shows coherent subject knowledge and accurate use of terminology, placing the work securely at the top of the 5â6 band.  A few uncritical source usages and minor conceptual slips preclude the absolute top mark, so 5 out of 6 is justified.

strengths: Good variety of clearly relevant financial data and industry analyses linked directly to the research question
Analysis interprets ratios and strategic models to draw logical causeâeffect connections between AI adoption and performance indicators
Arguments are well developed, with balanced discussion of risks and alternative explanations
weaknesses: Some analytical sections stop at correlations without fully integrating confounding factors (e.g. acquisition impacts, macro trends)
Counter-arguments and recommendations occasionally remain general rather than deeply substantiated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research quality and argumentation rise into the 7â9 band: evidence is consistently relevant, analysis is clear with well-supported conclusions, and the discussion demonstrates partially critical evaluation.  Remaining gaps in depth of evaluation and integration of counter-factors prevent a higher placement within the top band, so a score of 7 out of 12 is appropriate.

strengths: Report structure fully complies with EE requirements (title page, contents, headings, conclusion, references) in a clear and logical order
Layout and formattingâincluding page numbering, word count, consistent font, and figure captionsâare correctly applied
Figures and tables are generally clear and well integrated into the narrative
weaknesses: Minor errors remain (e.g. spelling of âMarriot,â occasional incomplete captions or sentence fragments)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required structural and formatting elements are present and correctly applied, and the organisation guides the reader effectively.  Small surface-level mistakes prevent flawless presentation, but the work fully meets the 3â4 band descriptors.  The strongest placement in that band (4 out of 4) is therefore justified.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question â âTo what extent does the introduction of generative AI affect Marriott hotelsâ profits and market position?â â is **explicit, narrowly bounded to one firm and two measurable outcomes**, and is reiterated throughout the essay.  Subsequent sections (methodology, main body tools) align directly with those two dimensions, evidencing a sustained focus.

Because the question still bundles two constructs and does not specify a time horizon or quantifiable metrics, some analytical precision is lost, preventing the highest mark.  Nevertheless, clarity and linkage to discussion remain strong, warranting the high band.

The conclusion synthesises findings and recognises methodological limits, and the essay offers **some balanced comments on risks (cyber-security, algorithmic flaws) and alternative explanations**.  Arguments progress logically, and tools are used to frame discussion.

Yet evaluation remains **partially critical**: counter-arguments are brief, and there is limited reflection on the reliability of financial attributions or on stakeholder trade-offs.  Recommendations are general rather than analytically derived.  These characteristics align with a secure 4 within the 4â6 band.

The essay employs appropriate course conceptsâAnsoff Matrix, Porterâs strategies, Balanced Scorecardâand relates them to Marriottâs AI adoption, showing **clear and largely coherent understanding**.  Definitions of generative AI and explanations of pricing algorithms, cost leadership, and differentiation demonstrate grasp of subject content.

Yet analysis sometimes slips into description, and causal links (AI â profit rise) are asserted rather than thoroughly substantiated, implying that conceptual understanding is partial rather than fully integrated.  This positions the work comfortably in the 3â4 band.

Throughout, the student employs appropriate business terminologyâ"dynamic pricing,â âoperational efficiency,â âmarket penetration,â âgross profit margin.â Usage is **mostly accurate and contextually suitable**, aiding clarity.

Minor errors (e.g., repeated misspelling "Marriot", occasional grammatical slips) and some inconsistent application of concept labels (e.g., confusing "product innovation" with service innovation) prevent fully consistent precision, placing performance within the upper portion of the 3â4 band.

Analytical sections interpret financial ratios, Ansoff positions and Porter strategies to outline **logical links between AI adoption and performance indicators**.  Calculations (market share, gross margin) and commentary on cause-effect relationships indicate emerging critical thinking.

However, analysis often stops at surface-level correlations; confounding factors (e.g., Starwood acquisition, macro trends) are acknowledged but not integrated into the evaluation. Numerical data are sometimes accepted at face value.  The analysis is therefore clear but not consistently well-developed, positioning it mid-band (4â6).

Most quotations, statistics and graphs are relevant to profits or market position and are referenced, indicating **generally appropriate application** of sources.  Visual data (net income, employee count, gross margin) are created and interpreted within the text.

However, several claims (e.g., a 17 % revenue boost solely from dynamic pricing) rely on secondary commentary without critical scrutiny, and some sources (blogs, Forbes opinion pieces) are treated uncritically.  The application is thus sound but not consistently rigorous, fitting the upper end of the 3â4 band.

The student outlines an entirely secondary-research design, identifies concrete source types (annual reports, academic journals, market reports) and justifies each Business-Management tool (Ansoff, Balanced Scorecard, etc.) with stated strengths and limitations. **Evidence of thoughtful, informed selection** is clear, and a broad range of corporate and academic materials underpins the study.

Limitations such as potential bias and lack of primary data are acknowledged, yet no mitigation (e.g., triangulation) is enacted, showing room for refinement; hence a strong but not perfect placement in the top band.

The student gathers a **good variety of published financial data, company reports, academic journals and industry analyses** that are clearly linked to the research question.  The evidence usedânet income trends, gross margin data, market-share calculationsâdemonstrates relevance and supports subsequent analysis.

Nevertheless, the study lacks primary data and offers limited cross-checking of source validity; some figures (e.g., âAI used in 150 waysâ) are cited without triangulation.  Overall, the depth and pertinence merit the 4â6 band, leaning toward the middle.

The layout conforms to EE conventions: title, contents table, sub-headings, images with captions, standard font size, and consistent referencing.  Page numbering, word count (3 987), and appropriate spacing are all present, demonstrating **correct application of formatting guidelines**.

Quality of figures is generally clear, though some images appear slightly pixelated and alt-text repeats unnecessarily.  Citations largely follow MLA style but contain occasional inconsistencies.  Overall compliance places the work securely in the 3â4 band at the lower end.

The student clearly states the organisational context, defines generative AI, and explains multiple operational applications (chat-bots, dynamic pricing, inventory management).  Relevant quantitative evidence (revenue, employee numbers, profit margins) and visual aids support the explanation, showing **accurate and detailed communication of the topicâs scope and significance**.

Key concepts such as personalisation, cost reduction and competitive positioning are woven into the narrative, demonstrating **coherent linkage between background information and the chosen Marriott case**. Minor lapses (spelling of âMarriotâ and some repetition) slightly detract from polish, thus the strand fits solidly in the 5â6 band but nearer the middle.

The essay follows the prescribed EE structureâtitle page with word count, abstract-style introduction, contents page, numbered headings, main body, conclusion, references and appendixâindicating **a clear and logical organisation** that guides the reader.

Minor issues (occasional long unbroken paragraphs, captions repeated beneath images) detract slightly from optimal flow, but all required elements are present and broadly effective.  This satisfies the descriptors of the 3â4 band, meriting a mid-band score.

annotations.comment: The chatbot example is effective, demonstrating clear application of AI concepts to customer service in relevant terminology.
The title effectively signals the focus but contains a spelling error (âMarriotâ); correct to âMarriottâ to maintain professional presentation.
The research question is clearly stated and tightly focused on measurable outcomes of profits and market position, providing a strong foundation for the essay.
The company history is detailed but somewhat overlong; consider condensing context to maintain focus on generative AIâs impact.
Definition and applications of generative AI are well-integrated with real examples, showing clear and appropriate use of terminology.
Figure of annual net income is clearly labelled and captioned; it effectively visualizes the financial trend since AI implementation.
Methodology outlines source types and tools but does not specify how secondary-data bias will be mitigated; add a triangulation strategy.
Initial Main Body paragraph remains largely descriptive; link operations description more directly to profit and market position analysis.
Word count is provided and within the 4 000-word limit, demonstrating compliance with formatting requirements.
The contents page lists all required sections and accurate page references, supporting a clear structural overview.
Indentation of sub-sections is inconsistent; consider aligning nested entries more precisely to enhance navigability.
Introduction offers comprehensive background on Marriottâs origins, scale, and recent revenue figures, demonstrating strong subject knowledge and context setting.
The Methodology section is clearly signposted with a distinct header, enhancing the reportâs organisational clarity.
Acknowledging potential bias in secondary sources demonstrates critical thinking and awareness of methodological limitations.
Introduce Theories and Tools by explicitly linking each to the research question to clarify their analytic relevance.
Listing each tool with stated limitations shows informed selection and understanding of theoretical strengths and weaknesses.
Choice of diverse analytical tools (Ansoff, market share, Balanced Scorecard, Porter) reflects comprehensive and relevant methodological design.
The opening sentence on page 6 is a fragment; revise to form a complete sentence and improve readability.
Main Body header is prominently placed, aiding reader orientation and structural flow.
Section on AI impact unifies consumer and stakeholder discussion, reflecting well-developed argument structure.
Claims about stakeholder benefits lack quantitative evidence; incorporate data (e.g., employee satisfaction scores) to substantiate assertions.
Detailed description of the Bonvoy appâs personalization features shows strong application of source material and concepts.
Employee count graph effectively displays trends but should clarify that figures are in thousands (000) to avoid misinterpretation.
Market penetration analysis links AI tools directly to customer engagement outcomes, reflecting logical analysis based on evidence.
Contextual narrative linking training programs to employee count increase demonstrates analytical depth and clear reasoning.
Investor discussion is introduced but cuts off mid-sentence; ensure complete explanations before transitioning to next topic.
Investment narrative shifts abruptly to share price changes; add transitional sentences to maintain coherent argument flow.
Introduction to the Ansoff Matrix is clear and well-defined, demonstrating proper use of theoretical concepts in context.
